  • Hide All Code

Project_dataclean

Zhan_Zhao

2026-3-31

Task Description

This R Markdown document reads, cleans, and explores the datasets used for the final project.
It focuses on diagnosed diabetic eye disease in a commercially insured population and examines variation across risk-factor subgroups.
It also incorporates state-level behavioral indicators from BRFSS to provide additional context.

The main tasks are:

  1. load and inspect the primary and supplementary datasets
  2. identify and define the primary diabetic eye disease outcome
  3. clean and simplify both datasets for state-level analysis
  4. select behavioral indicators from BRFSS and reshape them into a state-year summary file
  5. join the two datasets at the state-year level
  6. create descriptive summaries and exploratory visualizations
  7. document preliminary patterns for later discussion of limitations and future work

Initialize

define data directories

project.dir <- "/Users/zhaozhan/Project"
data.dir <- file.path(project.dir, "01 Data")
output.dir <- file.path(project.dir, "02 Output")

mscan.file <- file.path(
  data.dir,
  "Commercial_Medical_Insurance_(MSCANCC)_-_Vision_and_Eye_Health_Surveillance_20260331.csv"
)

brfss.file <- file.path(
  data.dir,
  "Nutrition,_Physical_Activity,_and_Obesity_-_Behavioral_Risk_Factor_Surveillance_System_20260331.csv"
)

Load and Inspect the Primary Dataset

#load_mscan
mscan <- read_csv(mscan.file, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names()

glimpse(mscan)
## Rows: 1,260,001
## Columns: 37
## $ year_start                 <dbl> 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2…
## $ year_end                   <dbl> 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2016, 2…
## $ location_abbr              <chr> "AL", "AL", "AK", "AK", "AK", "AK", "AK", "…

## $ data_source                <chr> "MSCANCC", "MSCANCC", "MSCANCC", "MSCANCC",…

## $ question                   <chr> "Annual prevalence of treated retinal detac…
## $ response                   <chr> "All Retinal detachment and defects", "All …

## $ sex_id                     <chr> "GALL", "GALL", "GF", "GF", "GF", "GF", "GM…

#insert_mscan_questions
mscan |>
  count(topic, sort = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   topic                      n
##   <chr>                  <int>
## 1 Eye Health Conditions 659458
## 2 Service Utilization   600543
mscan |>
  count(question, sort = TRUE) |>
  print(n = 50)

#insert_diabetic_eye_question
mscan |>
  filter(question == "Annual prevalence of diagnosed diabetic eye diseases") |>
  count(response, sort = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   response                                 n
##   <chr>                                <int>
## 1 Non-proliferative                     4710
## 2 Diabetic macular edema                4565
## 3 All Diabetic eye diseases             4534
## 4 Other diabetes related eye disorders  4446
## 5 Severe non-proliferative              4313
## 6 Proliferative diabetic retinopathy    4271
# Build the Primary Analytic Subset

diab_eye_state <- mscan |>
  filter(
    question == "Annual prevalence of diagnosed diabetic eye diseases",
    response == "All Diabetic eye diseases",
    geographic_level == "State",
    age == "All ages",
    sex == "Both sexes",
    race_ethnicity == "All races",
    risk_factor == "Diabetes",
    risk_factor_response == "Yes"
  ) |>
  transmute( 
    # select variables and rename it
    year_start,
    state_abbr,
    location_desc,
    response,
    diab_eye_prev = data_value,
    diab_eye_lcl = low_confidence_limit,
    diab_eye_ucl = high_confidence_limit,
    sample_size,
    numerator
  )

glimpse(diab_eye_state)
## Rows: 94
## Columns: 9
## $ year_start    <dbl> 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 20…
## $ state_abbr    <chr> "AK", "AL", "AR", "AZ", "CA", "CO", "CT", "FL", "GA", "I…
## $ location_desc <chr> "Alaska", "Alabama", "Arkansas", "Arizona", "California"…
## $ response      <chr> "All Diabetic eye diseases", "All Diabetic eye diseases"…
## $ diab_eye_prev <dbl> 7.08, 5.85, 6.60, 5.31, 7.22, 7.71, 7.06, 5.38, 5.74, 8.…

Load and Inspect the Supplementary Dataset

#load brfss
brfss <- read_csv(brfss.file, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names()

glimpse(brfss)
## Rows: 110,880
## Columns: 33
## $ year_start                  <dbl> 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, …
## $ year_end                    <dbl> 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, …
## $ location_abbr               <chr> "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", …
## $ location_desc               <chr> "Alabama", "Alabama", "Alabama", "Alabama"…
## $ datasource                  <chr> "Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance Syste…
## $ class                       <chr> "Obesity / Weight Status", "Obesity / Weig…
## $ topic                       <chr> "Obesity / Weight Status", "Obesity / Weig…
## $ question                    <chr> "Percent of adults aged 18 years and older…
## $ data_value_unit             <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA…
## $ data_value_type             <chr> "Value", "Value", "Value", "Value", "Value…
## $ data_value                  <dbl> 34.8, 35.8, 32.3, 34.1, 28.8, 16.3, 27.8, …
## $ data_value_alt              <dbl> 34.8, 35.8, 32.3, 34.1, 28.8, 16.3, 27.8, …
## $ data_value_footnote_symbol  <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA…
## $ data_value_footnote         <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA…
## $ low_confidence_limit        <dbl> 31.3, 31.1, 28.0, 29.7, 25.4, 12.6, 14.4, …
## $ high_confidence_limit       <dbl> 38.5, 40.8, 36.8, 38.8, 32.5, 20.9, 46.9, …
## $ sample_size                 <dbl> 1367, 757, 861, 785, 1125, 356, 58, 598, 8…
## $ total                       <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA…
## $ age_years                   <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "18 - 24", NA, "25 - 3…
## $ education                   <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA…
## $ sex                         <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA…
## $ income                      <chr> "$15,000 - $24,999", "$25,000 - $34,999", …
## $ race_ethnicity              <chr> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "2 or more races",…
## $ geo_location                <chr> "(32.840571122, -86.631860762)", "(32.8405…
## $ class_id                    <chr> "OWS", "OWS", "OWS", "OWS", "OWS", "OWS", …
## $ topic_id                    <chr> "OWS1", "OWS1", "OWS1", "OWS1", "OWS1", "O…
## $ question_id                 <chr> "Q036", "Q036", "Q036", "Q036", "Q036", "Q…
## $ data_value_type_id          <chr> "VALUE", "VALUE", "VALUE", "VALUE", "VALUE…
## $ location_id                 <chr> "01", "01", "01", "01", "01", "01", "01", …
## $ stratification_category1    <chr> "Income", "Income", "Income", "Income", "I…
## $ stratification1             <chr> "$15,000 - $24,999", "$25,000 - $34,999", …
## $ stratification_category_id1 <chr> "INC", "INC", "INC", "INC", "INC", "AGEYR"…
## $ stratification_id1          <chr> "INC1525", "INC2535", "INC3550", "INC5075"…
#insert brfss
brfss |>
  count(topic, sort = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   topic                                n
##   <chr>                            <int>
## 1 Physical Activity - Behavior     58520
## 2 Obesity / Weight Status          43120
## 3 Fruits and Vegetables - Behavior  9240
brfss |>
  count(question, sort = TRUE) |>
  print(n = 100)
## # A tibble: 9 × 2
##   question                                                                     n
##   <chr>                                                                    <int>
## 1 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who have an overweight classi… 21560
## 2 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who have obesity               21560
## 3 Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time physical activity        21560
## 4 Percent of adults who achieve at least 150 minutes a week of moderate-i…  9240
## 5 Percent of adults who achieve at least 150 minutes a week of moderate-i…  9240
## 6 Percent of adults who achieve more than 300 minutes a week of moderate-…  9240
## 7 Percent of adults who engage in muscle-strengthening activities on 2 or…  9240
## 8 Percent of adults who report consuming fruit less than one time daily     4620
## 9 Percent of adults who report consuming vegetables less than one time da…  4620
#Select BRFSS behavioral indicators
brfss_selected <- brfss |>
  filter(
    question %in% c(
      "Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who have obesity",
      "Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time physical activity"
    )
  )

brfss_selected |>
  count(question, stratification_category1, sort = TRUE) |>
  print(n = 100)
## # A tibble: 12 × 3
##    question                                         stratification_categ…¹     n
##    <chr>                                            <chr>                  <int>
##  1 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who h… Race/Ethnicity          6160
##  2 Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time… Race/Ethnicity          6160
##  3 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who h… Income                  5390
##  4 Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time… Income                  5390
##  5 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who h… Age (years)             4620
##  6 Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time… Age (years)             4620
##  7 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who h… Education               3080
##  8 Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time… Education               3080
##  9 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who h… Sex                     1540
## 10 Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time… Sex                     1540
## 11 Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who h… Total                    770
## 12 Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time… Total                    770
## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​stratification_category1
#Bulid brafss_state
brfss_state <- brfss_selected |>
  filter(stratification_category1 == "Total") |>
  select(
    year_start, location_abbr, location_desc,
    question, data_value,
    low_confidence_limit, high_confidence_limit,
    sample_size
  )

brfss_wide <- brfss_state |>
  mutate(
    behavior_indicator = case_when(
      question == "Percent of adults aged 18 years and older who have obesity" ~ "obesity_pct",
      question == "Percent of adults who engage in no leisure-time physical activity" ~ "inactive_pct",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  select(year_start, location_abbr, behavior_indicator, data_value) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = behavior_indicator,
    values_from = data_value
  )

glimpse(brfss_wide)
## Rows: 770
## Columns: 4
## $ year_start    <dbl> 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 2011, 20…
## $ location_abbr <chr> "AL", "AK", "AZ", "AR", "CA", "CO", "CT", "DE", "DC", "F…
## $ obesity_pct   <dbl> 32.0, 27.4, 25.1, 30.9, 23.8, 20.7, 24.5, 28.8, 23.7, 26…
## $ inactive_pct  <dbl> 32.6, 22.0, 24.1, 30.9, 19.1, 16.5, 25.5, 27.0, 19.8, 26…
head(brfss_wide)
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
##   year_start location_abbr obesity_pct inactive_pct
##        <dbl> <chr>               <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1       2011 AL                   32           32.6
## 2       2011 AK                   27.4         22  
## 3       2011 AZ                   25.1         24.1
## 4       2011 AR                   30.9         30.9
## 5       2011 CA                   23.8         19.1
## 6       2011 CO                   20.7         16.5

Join the Two Datasets

# Join data
joined_data <- diab_eye_state |>
  left_join(
    brfss_wide,
    by = c("state_abbr" = "location_abbr", "year_start" = "year_start")
  )

glimpse(joined_data)
## Rows: 94
## Columns: 11
## $ year_start    <dbl> 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 20…
## $ state_abbr    <chr> "AK", "AL", "AR", "AZ", "CA", "CO", "CT", "FL", "GA", "I…
## $ location_desc <chr> "Alaska", "Alabama", "Arkansas", "Arizona", "California"…
## $ response      <chr> "All Diabetic eye diseases", "All Diabetic eye diseases"…
## $ diab_eye_prev <dbl> 7.08, 5.85, 6.60, 5.31, 7.22, 7.71, 7.06, 5.38, 5.74, 8.…
## $ diab_eye_lcl  <dbl> 4.92, 5.55, 5.80, 4.98, 7.01, 7.14, 6.42, 5.22, 5.51, 7.…
## $ diab_eye_ucl  <dbl> 9.80, 6.16, 7.48, 5.65, 7.43, 8.32, 7.73, 5.55, 5.98, 9.…
## $ sample_size   <dbl> 470, 23300, 3500, 17300, 60600, 8000, 6000, 73100, 38500…
## $ numerator     <dbl> 30, 1400, 230, 920, 4400, 620, 430, 3900, 2200, 500, 150…
## $ obesity_pct   <dbl> 35.2, 39.2, 40.0, 31.9, 27.7, 24.9, 29.4, 30.1, 35.0, 37…
## $ inactive_pct  <dbl> 20.9, 29.1, 32.5, 21.3, 22.9, 17.5, 25.5, 25.4, 24.7, 24…
head(joined_data)
## # A tibble: 6 × 11
##   year_start state_abbr location_desc response        diab_eye_prev diab_eye_lcl
##        <dbl> <chr>      <chr>         <chr>                   <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1       2023 AK         Alaska        All Diabetic e…          7.08         4.92
## 2       2023 AL         Alabama       All Diabetic e…          5.85         5.55
## 3       2023 AR         Arkansas      All Diabetic e…          6.6          5.8 
## 4       2023 AZ         Arizona       All Diabetic e…          5.31         4.98
## 5       2023 CA         California    All Diabetic e…          7.22         7.01
## 6       2023 CO         Colorado      All Diabetic e…          7.71         7.14
## # ℹ 5 more variables: diab_eye_ucl <dbl>, sample_size <dbl>, numerator <dbl>,
## #   obesity_pct <dbl>, inactive_pct <dbl>
# Join quality summary
joined_data |>
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_states = n_distinct(state_abbr),
    n_years = n_distinct(year_start),
    missing_obesity = sum(is.na(obesity_pct)),
    missing_inactive = sum(is.na(inactive_pct)),
    complete_cases = sum(!is.na(diab_eye_prev) & !is.na(obesity_pct) & !is.na(inactive_pct))
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   n_rows n_states n_years missing_obesity missing_inactive complete_cases
##    <int>    <int>   <int>           <int>            <int>          <int>
## 1     94       48       2               5                5             89
# Check unmatched or incomplete records
joined_data |>
  filter(is.na(obesity_pct) | is.na(inactive_pct)) |>
  select(year_start, state_abbr, location_desc, obesity_pct, inactive_pct)
## # A tibble: 5 × 5
##   year_start state_abbr location_desc    obesity_pct inactive_pct
##        <dbl> <chr>      <chr>                  <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1       2023 KY         Kentucky                  NA           NA
## 2       2023 PA         Pennsylvania              NA           NA
## 3       2023 XX         Missing Location          NA           NA
## 4       2021 FL         Florida                   NA           NA
## 5       2021 XX         Missing Location          NA           NA
# Create complete-case analytic dataset
analysis_data <- joined_data |>
  drop_na(diab_eye_prev, obesity_pct, inactive_pct)

glimpse(analysis_data)
## Rows: 89
## Columns: 11
## $ year_start    <dbl> 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 20…
## $ state_abbr    <chr> "AK", "AL", "AR", "AZ", "CA", "CO", "CT", "FL", "GA", "I…
## $ location_desc <chr> "Alaska", "Alabama", "Arkansas", "Arizona", "California"…
## $ response      <chr> "All Diabetic eye diseases", "All Diabetic eye diseases"…
## $ diab_eye_prev <dbl> 7.08, 5.85, 6.60, 5.31, 7.22, 7.71, 7.06, 5.38, 5.74, 8.…
## $ diab_eye_lcl  <dbl> 4.92, 5.55, 5.80, 4.98, 7.01, 7.14, 6.42, 5.22, 5.51, 7.…
## $ diab_eye_ucl  <dbl> 9.80, 6.16, 7.48, 5.65, 7.43, 8.32, 7.73, 5.55, 5.98, 9.…
## $ sample_size   <dbl> 470, 23300, 3500, 17300, 60600, 8000, 6000, 73100, 38500…
## $ numerator     <dbl> 30, 1400, 230, 920, 4400, 620, 430, 3900, 2200, 500, 150…
## $ obesity_pct   <dbl> 35.2, 39.2, 40.0, 31.9, 27.7, 24.9, 29.4, 30.1, 35.0, 37…
## $ inactive_pct  <dbl> 20.9, 29.1, 32.5, 21.3, 22.9, 17.5, 25.5, 25.4, 24.7, 24…
head(analysis_data)
## # A tibble: 6 × 11
##   year_start state_abbr location_desc response        diab_eye_prev diab_eye_lcl
##        <dbl> <chr>      <chr>         <chr>                   <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1       2023 AK         Alaska        All Diabetic e…          7.08         4.92
## 2       2023 AL         Alabama       All Diabetic e…          5.85         5.55
## 3       2023 AR         Arkansas      All Diabetic e…          6.6          5.8 
## 4       2023 AZ         Arizona       All Diabetic e…          5.31         4.98
## 5       2023 CA         California    All Diabetic e…          7.22         7.01
## 6       2023 CO         Colorado      All Diabetic e…          7.71         7.14
## # ℹ 5 more variables: diab_eye_ucl <dbl>, sample_size <dbl>, numerator <dbl>,
## #   obesity_pct <dbl>, inactive_pct <dbl>

Data display

# Helper function to compute regression labels for plot annotations

build_lm_label <- function(df, x_var, y_var = "diab_eye_prev") {
  model_data <- df |>
    select(all_of(c(x_var, y_var))) |>
    drop_na()
  fit <- lm(reformulate(x_var, y_var), data = model_data)
  fit_summary <- summary(fit)
  # coef
  fit_coef <- coef(fit_summary)
  # 95% CI
  fit_ci <- confint(fit)
  slope_name <- x_var
  #slope value
  slope_est <- fit_coef[slope_name, "Estimate"]
  # P-value
  slope_p <- fit_coef[slope_name, "Pr(>|t|)"]
  # low ci
  slope_ci_low <- fit_ci[slope_name, 1]
  #high ci
  slope_ci_high <- fit_ci[slope_name, 2]
  r_sq <- fit_summary$r.squared
  paste0(
    "R² = ", sprintf("%.3f", r_sq),
    "\nSlope = ", sprintf("%.3f", slope_est),
    "\n95% CI = [", sprintf("%.3f", slope_ci_low), ", ", sprintf("%.3f", slope_ci_high), "]",
    "\np-value = ", format.pval(slope_p, digits = 3, eps = 0.001)
  )
}

# Build model labels
obesity_label <- build_lm_label(analysis_data, "obesity_pct")
inactive_label <- build_lm_label(analysis_data, "inactive_pct")

# Fit simple linear regression models
obesity_fit <- lm(diab_eye_prev ~ obesity_pct, data = analysis_data)
inactive_fit <- lm(diab_eye_prev ~ inactive_pct, data = analysis_data)

# Figure 1: State-level prevalence
p_state <- ggplot(
  analysis_data,
  aes(x = reorder(state_abbr, diab_eye_prev), y = diab_eye_prev, fill = diab_eye_prev)
) +
  geom_col(color = "gray25", alpha = 0.9) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#9ecae1",
    high = "#08519c",
    name = "Prevalence (%)"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "State-Level Prevalence of Diagnosed Diabetic Eye Disease",
    x = "State",
    y = "Crude prevalence (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_state

ggsave(
  file.path(output.dir, "figure1_state_prevalence.png"),
  plot = p_state,
  width = 9,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)

# Figure 2: Obesity and diabetic eye disease
p_obesity <- ggplot(analysis_data, aes(x = obesity_pct, y = diab_eye_prev)) +
  geom_point(color = "#2b8cbe", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#de2d26", fill = "#fcae91", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf,
    label = obesity_label,
    hjust = 1.05,
    vjust = 1.1,
    size = 3.3,
    label.size = 0.2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "State-Level Obesity and Diagnosed Diabetic Eye Disease",
    x = "Obesity prevalence (%)",
    y = "Diagnosed diabetic eye disease prevalence (%)",
    subtitle = "Linear fit with 95% confidence interval"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
## Warning in annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = obesity_label, hjust =
## 1.05, : Ignoring unknown parameters: `label.size`
p_obesity

ggsave(
  file.path(output.dir, "figure2_obesity_association.png"),
  plot = p_obesity,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

# Short interpretation for obesity model
obesity_coef <- summary(obesity_fit)$coefficients
obesity_ci <- confint(obesity_fit)

cat(
  "**Interpretation (Obesity):** A 1 percentage-point increase in state-level adult obesity prevalence was associated with an estimated ",
  sprintf("%.3f", obesity_coef["obesity_pct", "Estimate"]),
  " percentage-point change in diagnosed diabetic eye disease prevalence (95% CI: ",
  sprintf("%.3f", obesity_ci["obesity_pct", 1]), ", ",
  sprintf("%.3f", obesity_ci["obesity_pct", 2]),
  "; p-value ", format.pval(obesity_coef["obesity_pct", "Pr(>|t|)"], digits = 3, eps = 0.001),
  "). This ecological association should not be interpreted as an individual-level causal relationship.\n\n",
  sep = ""

)
## **Interpretation (Obesity):** A 1 percentage-point increase in state-level adult obesity prevalence was associated with an estimated -0.124 percentage-point change in diagnosed diabetic eye disease prevalence (95% CI: -0.202, -0.047; p-value 0.00199). This ecological association should not be interpreted as an individual-level causal relationship.
# Figure 3: Physical inactivity and diabetic eye disease
p_inactive <- ggplot(analysis_data, aes(x = inactive_pct, y = diab_eye_prev)) +
  geom_point(color = "#31a354", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.75) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#756bb1", fill = "#dadaeb", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = inactive_label,
    hjust = 1.05,
    vjust = 1.1,
    size = 3.3,
    label.size = 0.2
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Physical Inactivity and Diagnosed Diabetic Eye Disease",
    x = "No leisure-time physical activity (%)",
    y = "Diagnosed diabetic eye disease prevalence (%)",
    subtitle = "Linear fit with 95% confidence interval"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
## Warning in annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = inactive_label, hjust =
## 1.05, : Ignoring unknown parameters: `label.size`
p_inactive

ggsave(
  file.path(output.dir, "figure3_inactivity_association.png"),
  plot = p_inactive,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

# Short interpretation for physical inactivity model
inactive_coef <- summary(inactive_fit)$coefficients
inactive_ci <- confint(inactive_fit)

cat(
  "**Interpretation (Physical inactivity):** A 1 percentage-point increase in state-level physical inactivity prevalence was associated with an estimated ",
  sprintf("%.3f", inactive_coef["inactive_pct", "Estimate"]),
  " percentage-point change in diagnosed diabetic eye disease prevalence (95% CI: ",
  sprintf("%.3f", inactive_ci["inactive_pct", 1]), ", ",
  sprintf("%.3f", inactive_ci["inactive_pct", 2]),
  "; p-value ", format.pval(inactive_coef["inactive_pct", "Pr(>|t|)"], digits = 3, eps = 0.001),
  "). This ecological association should not be interpreted as an individual-level causal relationship.\n",
  sep = ""
)
## **Interpretation (Physical inactivity):** A 1 percentage-point increase in state-level physical inactivity prevalence was associated with an estimated -0.143 percentage-point change in diagnosed diabetic eye disease prevalence (95% CI: -0.218, -0.068; p-value <0.001). This ecological association should not be interpreted as an individual-level causal relationship.

Correlation Analysis & Heatmap

# Correlation heatmap for final analytic variables

#Create complete-case data for correlation analysis

cor_data <- analysis_data |>
  select(
    diab_eye_prev,
    obesity_pct,
    inactive_pct
  ) |>
  drop_na()

#Rename Variables for clearer plot labels
cor_data_labeled <- cor_data |>
  rename(
    `Diagnosed diabetic eye disease` = diab_eye_prev,
    `Adult obesity` = obesity_pct,
    `Physical inactivity` = inactive_pct
  )

#Create Pearson correlation matirx

corr_mat <- round(
  cor(
    cor_data_labeled,
    use = "complete.obs",
    method = "pearson"),
  2
)

corr_mat
##                                Diagnosed diabetic eye disease Adult obesity
## Diagnosed diabetic eye disease                           1.00         -0.32
## Adult obesity                                           -0.32          1.00
## Physical inactivity                                     -0.38          0.69
##                                Physical inactivity
## Diagnosed diabetic eye disease               -0.38
## Adult obesity                                 0.69
## Physical inactivity                           1.00
#convert correlation matrix to long format
corr_heatmap_data <- as.data.frame(as.table(corr_mat)) |>
  rename(
    variable_1 = Var1,
    variable_2 = Var2,
    correlation = Freq
  )

#Figure4: Correlation heatmap
p_corr_heatmap <- ggplot(
  corr_heatmap_data,
  aes(
    x = variable_1,
    y = variable_2,
    fill = correlation)
  )+
    geom_tile(color = "white")+
    geom_text(
      aes(
        label = sprintf("%.2f",correlation)),
        color = "black",
        size = 4
      )+
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "#b2182b",
      mid = "white",
      high = "#2166ac",
      midpoint = 0,
      limits = c(-1,1),
      name = "Pearson r"
    )+
    labs(
      title = "Correlation Heatmap of Diabetic Eye Disease and Behavioral Indicators",
      x = "",
      y = ""
    )+
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

p_corr_heatmap

ggsave(
  file.path(output.dir, "figure4_correlation_heatmap.png"),
  plot = p_corr_heatmap,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

# Correlation tests with p-values

cor_obesity <- cor.test(
  analysis_data$diab_eye_prev,
  analysis_data$obesity_pct,
  method = "pearson"
)

cor_inactive <- cor.test(
  analysis_data$diab_eye_prev,
  analysis_data$inactive_pct,
  method = "pearson"
)

cor_summary <- tibble(
  predictor = c("Adult obesity", "Physical inactivity"),
  correlation_r = c(
    unname(cor_obesity$estimate),
    unname(cor_inactive$estimate)
  ),
  p_value = c(
    cor_obesity$p.value,
    cor_inactive$p.value
  )
)

cor_summary
## # A tibble: 2 × 3
##   predictor           correlation_r  p_value
##   <chr>                       <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 Adult obesity              -0.323 0.00199 
## 2 Physical inactivity        -0.376 0.000285
write_csv(
  cor_summary,
  file.path(output.dir, "correlation_summary.csv")
)
